text:
In the first image, the quiet, sterile hallway suggests a transition—a liminal space with glass boundaries reflecting my uncertainty. I feel expectant, a little detached, noticing how open yet impersonal it is. The fire equipment on the wall is a reminder of potential hazards, both literal and metaphorical, that mark institutional life.

In the second image, surrounded by bold colors and innovative displays, I sense a shift toward curiosity and reflection. The text on the screen captures my unease: exposed, misunderstood, but also searching for meaning. Here, the philosophy of perception and intention comes alive—I am aware of being observed and observing at the same time. This brings both a vulnerability and an odd sense of belonging in a space dedicated to research and development—a perfect metaphor for self-exploration.
